behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real filesystem watch recursion/path delivery, not just local handler logic. MOCK-MISS: bug appears when watcher + OS event stream + pipeline wiring interact. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE are both yes: mocked watcher events can falsely pass while real recursive watch setup misses nested paths. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow rejected because behavior text is purely filesystem daemon behavior with no AI/model step.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon against a real temp directory tree. Create `root/sub/a.txt` after startup. Assert one successful pipeline output appears in output dir for that nested file path. Validate this with real OS file events (no mocked watcher).

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is primarily local decision logic (path+time-window check), and can fail within one object/function boundary. MOCK-MISS: no required multi-module boundary in wording. REAL-INFRA: no. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no. Rejection gate: Integration/System rejected because behavior text does not require real watcher/OS events; it is explicitly dedup policy for repeated requests.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate dedup component with configurable window and fake clock. Submit same normalized file path twice within window, assert second request is rejected; advance clock beyond window, assert request is accepted. Include path canonicalization edge case used by dedup keying.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly names OS multi-event emission; failure appears in real watcher/event semantics and debounce/dedup interaction. REAL-INFRA yes by definition. MOCK-HIDE yes: synthetic single-event mocks miss duplicate-event bursts. Rejection gate: Integration rejected because text requires real OS event behavior; Agentic/Workflow rejected due no AI stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon with real watched directory and perform a write pattern known to trigger multiple events (create + append + close/rename sequence). Capture processing outputs/logs and assert exactly one processing invocation/output for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: Directory deletion/recreation watcher recovery is OS/filesystem primitive behavior plus watcher re-subscription logic. REAL-INFRA yes; MOCK-HIDE yes because fake watcher callbacks do not reproduce watch-handle invalidation semantics. Rejection gate: Integration rejected since behavior text requires live directory removal/recreation while process runs.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon watching real temp dir. Delete watched dir, recreate it at same path, then place a new file. Assert daemon processes post-recreation file without daemon restart and without manual reconfiguration.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Ordering depends on real filesystem timestamps and concurrent arrival timing. Although queue sort logic is testable in isolation, this behavior’s failure mode includes metadata resolution/tie behavior from real FS plus daemon ingestion timing. REAL-INFRA likely yes; MOCK-HIDE likely yes for timestamp precision/ordering anomalies. Rejection gate: Integration considered but rejected because behavior text anchors order to creation timestamps at arrival time, which are OS-provided.
behaviors.test_description: With daemon running on real dir, create multiple files in rapid succession with controlled timestamp differences (and a tie case). Assert processing order matches filesystem creation-time ordering policy, including deterministic tie-breaker if defined.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: This is a real timing/IO boundary between writer process, OS file events, and daemon readiness checks. REAL-INFRA yes. MOCK-HIDE yes: in-memory fakes do not reproduce partial-write visibility and event timing races. Rejection gate: Integration rejected because behavior explicitly depends on write-in-progress state at event time; Agentic/Workflow rejected due no model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon and a writer that writes a large file in chunks with delays. Ensure arrival event occurs before final chunk. Assert daemon does not process partial content and only processes after write completion (e.g., final size/hash observed).
